_comment: Per-1M-token rates (per-1M-character for DeepL). MUST be re-verified against each vendor's current pricing page before trusting any cost figure in results.md — pricing changes often and nothing here is auto-synced. Use null for any rate you haven't personally verified: the report will show 'rate not set' instead of a misleading $0.00.

gemini-2.5-pro._verified: ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/pricing, checked 2026-07-17. This is the <=200k-token-prompt tier; prompts over 200k tokens bill at 2.50 / 15.00 / 0.25 instead, which this file does not model.

deepseek-v4-flash._verified: api-docs.deepseek.com/quick_start/pricing, checked 2026-07-17. inputPer1M is the cache-miss rate; cachedInputPer1M is the cache-hit rate.

deepseek-v4-pro._verified: api-docs.deepseek.com/quick_start/pricing, checked 2026-07-17. inputPer1M is the cache-miss rate; cachedInputPer1M is the cache-hit rate.

qwen-plus._note: Alibaba Cloud Model Studio pricing is region-specific — check your console's pricing page.

qwen-max._note: Alibaba Cloud Model Studio pricing is region-specific — check your console's pricing page.

azure-openai._note: No single universal rate — depends on your specific Azure deployment/region/tier. Fill in from your own Azure pricing page, not a generic OpenAI rate card.

deepl._note: deepl.com/pro-api's API rate table did not render via automated fetch (likely client-side rendered) — fill in the current per-character rate manually before trusting DeepL cost figures.

gpt-5-mini._verified: developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5-mini, checked 2026-07-20. This is OpenAI's own direct-API rate, NOT independently confirmed against Azure — the model has zero matching SKUs anywhere in Microsoft's official Retail Prices API (prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices), checked the same day, any region. Azure's 'Global Standard' tier is widely documented to price these OpenAI-family models at parity with OpenAI's direct API, but that parity itself isn't independently verified here — treat as a strong estimate, confirm against your Foundry resource's actual bill before trusting.

gpt-5-nano._verified: developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5-nano, checked 2026-07-20. Same caveat as gpt-5-mini above: OpenAI direct-API rate, assumed at parity with Azure Global Standard, not independently confirmed against Azure (zero matching SKU in the Retail Prices API, any region, checked same day).

gpt-5-chat._verified: developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5-chat-latest, checked 2026-07-20. Same caveat as gpt-5-mini above — OpenAI direct-API rate (identical to gpt-5.1's, see that entry), not independently confirmed against Azure (zero matching SKU in the Retail Prices API, any region).

gpt-5.1._verified: developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5.1, checked 2026-07-20. Same caveat as gpt-5-mini above — OpenAI direct-API rate, not independently confirmed against Azure (zero matching SKU in the Retail Prices API, any region). Notably identical to gpt-5-chat's rate.

gpt-4.1._verified: developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-4.1, checked 2026-07-20 — OpenAI's own direct-API rate. Cross-checked against Microsoft's official Azure Retail Prices API the same day: gpt-4.1 DOES appear there, but only as fine-tuning meters (FT Training/hosting/cached-input) — no base/inference consumption meter exists in that catalog at all, so Azure-specific confirmation isn't possible via that source. Assumed at Global Standard parity with this OpenAI rate; confirm against your Foundry resource's bill before trusting.

gpt-4.1-nano._verified: developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-4.1-nano, checked 2026-07-20. Same caveat as gpt-4.1 above — OpenAI direct-API rate; Azure's Retail Prices API has only fine-tuning meters for this model, no base consumption meter to cross-check against.

o3._verified: prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Microsoft's official Azure Retail Prices API), armRegionName='australiaeast', currencyCode=USD, checked 2026-07-20. Meters: 'o3 0416 Inp glbl' $0.002/1K, 'o3 0416 Outp glbl' $0.008/1K, 'o3 0416 cached Inp glbl' $0.0005/1K — converted to per-1M here. This is the base o3 meter, NOT o3-mini or o3-pro (both priced differently and also present in the region). Unlike every other Foundry model in this file, this one IS a real Azure-specific meter, not an OpenAI-direct-rate assumption.

Kimi-K2.6._verified: azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/ai-foundry-models/kimi/ (official Microsoft Foundry Models pricing page for Kimi), via search-engine content extraction checked 2026-07-20 — direct WebFetch of this page timed out (heavy client-rendering), so this is grounded in the page's indexed content rather than a raw fetch I could re-verify byte-for-byte myself. Zero matching SKU in the Azure Retail Prices API itself (prices.azure.com) — third-party Foundry catalog models appear to be billed via Azure Marketplace metering, which that API doesn't expose.

Kimi-K2.5._note: STILL UNVERIFIED after a second check, 2026-07-20 — unlike Kimi-K2.6 (confirmed via Microsoft's official Foundry-Kimi pricing page), no official source for K2.5's specific rate turned up in two search rounds. These input/output numbers are unconfirmed; cached is still null. Check your Foundry resource's bill directly.

DeepSeek-V4-Flash._note: STILL UNVERIFIED after a second check, 2026-07-20 — unlike DeepSeek-V4-Pro (see that entry), no official source for Flash's specific rate turned up. Also: a Microsoft Q&A thread reports DeepSeek-V4-Pro billing on Foundry running ~4.5x DeepSeek's own direct-API rate — do NOT treat the lowercase 'deepseek-v4-flash' entry above as a substitute estimate for this one; they are billed through different mechanisms.

DeepSeek-V4-Pro._verified: azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/ai-foundry-models/deepseek/ (official Microsoft Foundry Models pricing page for DeepSeek), via search-engine content extraction checked 2026-07-20 — same direct-WebFetch-timeout caveat as Kimi-K2.6 above. Input adjusted from a previously-entered 1.74 to 1.75 to match this source; output (3.48) already matched. Zero matching SKU in the Azure Retail Prices API itself — see Kimi-K2.6's note on likely Marketplace billing. Also see the Microsoft Q&A thread on this exact model's Foundry-vs-direct-API billing discrepancy (~4.5x) — this Foundry-specific rate is the correct one to use here regardless, just flagging the discrepancy exists.
